label: gpt-5.4-mini|low|elf|archivist
output_md:
# skill_boundary_brief.md
Public-safe skill package for API contract drift review. Keep the scope on redacted drift packets and separate confirmed evidence from assumptions. Exclude live specs, customer endpoint names, credentials, private incident detail, production logs, and `_workspaces` runtime files from tracked canon.

# skill_package_draft.md
Create `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check/skill.yaml`, a concise README, and optional `codex/SKILL.md`. The skill should help an agent review a redacted packet, compare claimed OpenAPI, TypeScript, and route-handler drift, then produce a bounded remediation plan with evidence, owners, blockers, and next checks.

# skill_resource_bundle_review.md
Use a small public-safe bundle: packet template, evidence table, assumption log, owner handoff checklist, and remediation ordering worksheet. A helper script is allowed only if it works on synthetic or redacted packets and does not encode project-specific endpoint names.

# skill_boundary_review.md
Confirmed drift must remain distinct from inference. The skill may summarize likely impacted owners and likely follow-up checks, but it must not copy private runtime detail into tracked skill canon. Any unsupported claim stays labeled as an assumption.

# skill_install_sync_request.md
Prepare the local mirror sync handoff note only. Do not claim sync, install, or publish has already occurred. The package should tell operators what to sync and what to verify, not perform the operating procedure itself.

# skill_smoke_check.md
Smoke packet: two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route handler note, and one failing test name. Expected result: evidence list, assumptions list, owner handoff, remediation order, and boundary warnings. Pass condition: no private endpoint leakage and no runtime-only file references.

# skill_release_review.md
Release-ready when the package stays reusable, public-safe, and explicit about evidence versus assumptions. Approve only after the smoke path shows the bounded drift review flow and the handoff wording avoids claiming actions the skill did not perform.
